heading: Energy storage competitiveness
label: 17231 Energy storage competitiveness

children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “United States Energy Storage Competitiveness Act of 2007”.

children.heading: Council
children.label: 1 Council
children.text: The term “Council” means the Energy Storage Advisory Council established under subsection (e).

children.heading: Compressed air energy storage
children.label: 2 Compressed air energy storage
children.text: The term “compressed air energy storage” means, in the case of an electricity grid application, the storage of energy through the compression of air.

children.label: A
children.text: a vehicle that uses an electric motor for all or part of the motive power of the vehicle, including battery electric, hybrid electric, plug-in hybrid electric, fuel cell, and plug-in fuel cell vehicles and rail transportation vehicles; or

children.label: B
children.text: mobile equipment that uses an electric motor to replace an internal combustion engine for all or part of the work of the equipment.

children.heading: Islanding
children.label: 4 Islanding
children.text: The term “islanding” means a distributed generator or energy storage device continuing to power a location in the absence of electric power from the primary source.

children.heading: Flywheel
children.label: 5 Flywheel
children.text: The term “flywheel” means, in the case of an electricity grid application, a device used to store rotational kinetic energy.

children.heading: Microgrid
children.label: 6 Microgrid
children.text: The term “microgrid” means an integrated energy system consisting of interconnected loads and distributed energy resources (including generators and energy storage devices), which as an integrated system can operate in parallel with the utility grid or in an intentional islanding mode.

children.heading: Self-healing grid
children.label: 7 Self-healing grid
children.text: The term “self-healing grid” means a grid that is capable of automatically anticipating and responding to power system disturbances (including the isolation of failed sections and components), while optimizing the performance and service of the grid to customers.

children.heading: Spinning reserve services
children.label: 8 Spinning reserve services
children.text: The term “spinning reserve services” means a quantity of electric generating capacity in excess of the quantity needed to meet peak electric demand.

children.heading: Ultracapacitor
children.label: 9 Ultracapacitor
children.text: The term “ultracapacitor” means an energy storage device that has a power density comparable to a conventional capacitor but is capable of exceeding the energy density of a conventional capacitor by several orders of magnitude.

children.heading: Program
children.label: c Program
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a research, development, and demonstration program to support the ability of the United States to remain globally competitive in energy storage systems for electric drive vehicles, stationary applications, and electricity transmission and distribution.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: d Coordination
children.text: In carrying out the activities of this section, the Secretary shall coordinate relevant efforts with appropriate Federal agencies, including the Department of Transportation.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary shall establish an Energy Storage Advisory Council.December 19, 2007

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Council shall consist of not less than 15 individuals appointed by the Secretary, based on recommendations of the National Academy of Sciences.

children.heading: Energy storage industry
children.label: B Energy storage industry
children.text: The Council shall consist primarily of representatives of the energy storage industry of the United States.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: C Chairperson
children.text: The Secretary shall select a Chairperson for the Council from among the members appointed under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Council shall meet not less than once a year.

children.heading: Chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: B Chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall apply to a meeting of the Council.

children.heading: Plans
children.label: 4 Plans
children.text: No later than 1 year after , and every 5 years thereafter, the Council, in conjunction with the Secretary, shall develop a 5-year plan for integrating basic and applied research so that the United States retains a globally competitive domestic energy storage industry for electric drive vehicles, stationary applications, and electricity transmission and distribution.December 19, 2007

children.label: A
children.text: assess, every 2 years, the performance of the Department in meeting the goals of the plans developed under paragraph (4); and

children.label: B
children.text: make specific recommendations to the Secretary on programs or activities that should be established or terminated to meet those goals.

children.label: A
children.text: materials design;

children.label: B
children.text: materials synthesis and characterization;

children.label: C
children.text: electrode-active materials, including electrolytes and bioelectrolytes;

children.label: D
children.text: surface and interface dynamics;

children.label: E
children.text: modeling and simulation; and

children.label: F
children.text: thermal behavior and life degradation mechanisms.

children.heading: Nanoscience centers
children.label: 2 Nanoscience centers
children.text: The Secretary, in cooperation with the Council, shall coordinate the activities of the nanoscience centers of the Department to help the energy storage research centers of the Department maintain a globally competitive posture in energy storage systems for electric drive vehicles, stationary applications, and electricity transmission and distribution.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 3 Funding
children.text: For activities carried out under this subsection, in addition to funding activities at National Laboratories, the Secretary shall award funds to, and coordinate activities with, a range of stakeholders including the public, private, and academic sectors.

children.label: A
children.text: ultracapacitors;

children.label: B
children.text: flywheels;

children.label: C
children.text: batteries and battery systems (including flow batteries);

children.label: D
children.text: compressed air energy systems;

children.label: E
children.text: power conditioning electronics;

children.label: F
children.text: manufacturing technologies for energy storage systems;

children.label: G
children.text: thermal management systems; and

children.label: H
children.text: hydrogen as an energy storage medium.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 2 Funding
children.text: For activities carried out under this subsection, in addition to funding activities at National Laboratories, the Secretary shall provide funds to, and coordinate activities with, a range of stakeholders, including the public, private, and academic sectors.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish, through competitive bids, not more than 4 energy storage research centers to translate basic research into applied technologies to advance the capability of the United States to maintain a globally competitive posture in energy storage systems for electric drive vehicles, stationary applications, and electricity transmission and distribution.

children.heading: Program management
children.label: 2 Program management
children.text: The centers shall be managed by the Under Secretary for Science of the Department.

children.heading: Participation agreements
children.label: 3 Participation agreements
children.text: As a condition of participating in a center, a participant shall enter into a participation agreement with the center that requires that activities conducted by the participant for the center promote the goal of enabling the United States to compete successfully in global energy storage markets.

children.heading: Plans
children.label: 4 Plans
children.text: A center shall conduct activities that promote the achievement of the goals of the plans of the Council under subsection (e)(4).

children.heading: National laboratories
children.label: 5 National laboratories
children.text: A national laboratory (as defined in ) may participate in a center established under this subsection, including a cooperative research and development agreement (as defined in ).section 15801 of this titlesection 3710a(d) of title 15

children.heading: Disclosure
children.label: 6 Disclosure
children.text: may apply to any project carried out through a grant, contract, or cooperative agreement under this subsection.Section 13293 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: if an industrial participant is active in a energy storage research center established under this subsection relating to the advancement of energy storage technologies carried out, in whole or in part, with Federal funding, the industrial participant be granted the first option to negotiate with the invention owner, at least in the field of energy storage technologies, nonexclusive licenses, and royalties on terms that are reasonable, as determined by the Secretary;1So in original. Probably should be “an”.1

children.label: i
children.text: the patent holder shall not negotiate any license or royalty agreement with any entity that is not an industrial participant under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the patent holder shall negotiate nonexclusive licenses and royalties in good faith with any interested industrial participant under this subsection; and

children.label: C
children.text: the new invention be developed under such other terms as the Secretary determines to be necessary to promote the accelerated commercialization of inventions made under this subsection to advance the capability of the United States to successfully compete in global energy storage markets.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program of new demonstrations of advanced energy storage systems.

children.label: A
children.text: be regionally diversified; and

children.label: B
children.text: expand on the existing technology demonstration program of the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: rural electric cooperatives;

children.label: B
children.text: investor owned utilities;

children.label: C
children.text: municipally owned electric utilities;

children.label: D
children.text: energy storage systems manufacturers;

children.label: E
children.text: electric drive vehicle manufacturers;

children.label: F
children.text: the renewable energy production industry;

children.label: G
children.text: State or local energy offices;

children.label: H
children.text: the fuel cell industry; and

children.label: I
children.text: institutions of higher education.

children.label: A
children.text: Energy storage to improve the feasibility of microgrids or islanding, or transmission and distribution capability, to improve reliability in rural areas.

children.label: B
children.text: Integration of an energy storage system with a self-healing grid.

children.label: C
children.text: Use of energy storage to improve security to emergency response infrastructure and ensure availability of emergency backup power for consumers.

children.label: D
children.text: Integration with a renewable energy production source, at the source or away from the source.

children.label: E
children.text: Use of energy storage to provide ancillary services, such as spinning reserve services, for grid management.

children.label: F
children.text: Advancement of power conversion systems to make the systems smarter, more efficient, able to communicate with other inverters, and able to control voltage.

children.label: G
children.text: Use of energy storage to optimize transmission and distribution operation and power quality, which could address overloaded lines and maintenance of transformers and substations.

children.label: H
children.text: Use of advanced energy storage for peak load management of homes, businesses, and the grid.

children.label: I
children.text: Use of energy storage devices to store energy during nonpeak generation periods to make better use of existing grid assets.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program of electric drive vehicle energy storage technology demonstrations.

children.label: A
children.text: energy storage systems manufacturers and suppliers of the manufacturers;

children.label: B
children.text: electric drive vehicle manufacturers;

children.label: C
children.text: rural electric cooperatives;

children.label: D
children.text: investor owned utilities;

children.label: E
children.text: municipal and rural electric utilities;

children.label: F
children.text: State and local governments;

children.label: G
children.text: metropolitan transportation authorities; and

children.label: H
children.text: institutions of higher education.

children.label: A
children.text: Novel, high capacity, high efficiency energy storage, charging, and control systems, along with the collection of data on performance characteristics, such as battery life, energy storage capacity, and power delivery capacity.

children.label: B
children.text: Advanced onboard energy management systems and highly efficient battery cooling systems.

children.label: C
children.text: Integration of those systems on a prototype vehicular platform, including with drivetrain systems for passenger, commercial, and nonroad electric drive vehicles.

children.label: D
children.text: New technologies and processes that reduce manufacturing costs.

children.label: E
children.text: Integration of advanced vehicle technologies with electricity distribution system and smart metering technology.

children.label: F
children.text: Control systems that minimize emissions profiles in cases in which clean diesel engines are part of a plug-in hybrid drive system.

children.heading: Electric drive vehicle battery second-life applications and recycling
children.label: k Electric drive vehicle battery second-life applications and recycling

children.heading: Battery recycling and second-life applications program
children.label: A Battery recycling and second-life applications program
children.text: The term “battery recycling and second-life applications program” means the electric drive vehicle battery recycling and second-life applications program established under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Critical material
children.label: B Critical material
children.text: The term “critical material” has the meaning given the term in .section 1606(a) of title 30

children.heading: Economically distressed area
children.label: C Economically distressed area
children.text: The term “economically distressed area” means an area described in .section 3161(a) of this title

children.heading: Electric drive vehicle battery
children.label: D Electric drive vehicle battery
children.text: The term “electric vehicle battery” means any battery that is a motive power source for an electric drive vehicle.drive2So in original.2

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: E Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means an entity described in any of paragraphs (1) through (5) of .section 16353(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: second-life applications for electric drive vehicle batteries that have been used to power electric drive vehicles; and

children.label: B
children.text: technologies and processes for final recycling and disposal of the devices described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Electric drive vehicle battery recycling and second-life applications
children.label: 3 Electric drive vehicle battery recycling and second-life applications
i
children.text: award grants under subparagraph (D); and

children.label: ii
children.text: carry out other activities in accordance with this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: To improve the recycling rates and second-use adoption rates of electric drive vehicle batteries.

children.label: ii
children.text: To optimize the design and adaptability of electric drive vehicle batteries to make electric drive vehicle batteries more easily recyclable.

children.label: iii
children.text: To establish alternative supply chains for critical materials that are found in electric drive vehicle batteries.

children.label: iv
children.text: To reduce the cost of manufacturing, installation, purchase, operation, and maintenance of electric drive vehicle batteries.

children.label: v
children.text: To improve the environmental impact of electric drive vehicle battery recycling processes.

children.heading: Targets
children.label: C Targets
children.text: In carrying out the battery recycling and second-life applications program, the Secretary shall address near-term (up to 2 years), mid-term (up to 5 years), and long-term (up to 10 years) challenges to the recycling of electric drive vehicle batteries.

children.label: I
children.text: to conduct research, development, testing, and evaluation of solutions to increase the rate and productivity of electric drive vehicle battery recycling; and

children.label: aa
children.text: technology to increase the efficiency of electric drive vehicle battery recycling and maximize the recovery of critical materials for use in new products;

children.label: bb
children.text: expanded uses for critical materials recovered from electric drive vehicle batteries;

children.label: cc
children.text: product design and construction to facilitate the disassembly and recycling of electric drive vehicle batteries;

children.label: dd
children.text: product design and construction and other tools and techniques to extend the lifecycle of electric drive vehicle batteries, including methods to promote the safe second-use of electric drive vehicle batteries;

children.label: ee
children.text: strategies to increase consumer acceptance of, and participation in, the recycling of electric drive vehicle batteries;

children.label: ff
children.text: improvements and changes to electric drive vehicle battery chemistries that include ways to decrease processing costs for battery recycling without sacrificing front-end performance;

children.label: gg
children.text: second-use of electric drive vehicle batteries, including in applications outside of the automotive industry; and

children.label: hh
children.text: the commercialization and scale-up of electric drive vehicle battery recycling technologies.

children.label: I
children.text: are located in geographically diverse regions of the United States;

children.label: II
children.text: include business commercialization plans that have the potential for the recycling of electric drive vehicle batteries at high volumes;

children.label: III
children.text: support the development of advanced manufacturing technologies that have the potential to improve the competitiveness of the United States in the international electric drive vehicle battery manufacturing sector;

children.label: IV
children.text: provide the greatest potential to reduce costs for consumers and promote accessibility and community implementation of demonstrated technologies;

children.label: V
children.text: increase disclosure and transparency of information to consumers;

children.label: VI
children.text: support the development or demonstration of projects in economically distressed areas; and

children.label: VII
children.text: support other relevant priorities, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Solicitation
children.label: iii Solicitation
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall conduct a national solicitation for applications for grants described in clause (i).November 15, 2021

children.label: I
children.text: best practices relating to those grants, for use in the electric drive vehicle battery manufacturing, design, installation, refurbishing, or recycling industries;

children.label: II
children.text: coordination with information dissemination programs relating to general recycling of electronic devices; and

children.label: III
children.text: educational materials for the public, produced in conjunction with State and local governments or nonprofit organizations, on the problems and solutions relating to the recycling and second-life applications of electric drive vehicle batteries.

children.heading: Coordination with other programs of the Department
children.label: E Coordination with other programs of the Department
children.text: In carrying out the battery recycling and second-life applications program, the Secretary shall coordinate and leverage the resources of complementary efforts of the Department.

children.heading: Study
children.label: i Study
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a study on the viable market opportunities available for the recycling, second-use, and manufacturing of electric drive vehicle batteries in the United States.

children.label: I
children.text: the ability of relevant businesses or other entities to competitively manufacture electric drive vehicle batteries and recycle electric drive vehicle batteries in the United States;

children.label: II
children.text: any existing electric drive vehicle battery recycling and second-use practices and plans of electric drive vehicle manufacturing companies in the United States;

children.label: III
children.text: any barriers to electric drive vehicle battery recycling in the United States;

children.label: IV
children.text: opportunities and barriers in electric drive vehicle battery supply chains in the United States and internationally, including with allies and trading partners;

children.label: V
children.text: opportunities for job creation in the electric drive vehicle battery recycling and manufacturing fields and the necessary skills employees must acquire for growth of those fields in the United States;

children.label: VI
children.text: policy recommendations for enhancing electric drive vehicle battery manufacturing and recycling in the United States;

children.label: VII
children.text: any recommendations for lowering logistics costs and creating better coordination and efficiency with respect to the removal, collection, transportation, storage, and disassembly of electric drive vehicle batteries;

children.label: VIII
children.text: any recommendations for areas of coordination with other Federal agencies to improve electric drive vehicle battery recycling rates in the United States;

children.label: IX
children.text: an aggressive 2-year target and plan, the implementation of which shall begin during the 90-day period beginning on the date on which the report is submitted, to enhance the competitiveness of electric drive vehicle battery manufacturing and recycling in the United States; and

children.label: X
children.text: needs for future research, development, and demonstration projects in electric drive vehicle battery manufacturing, recycling, and related areas, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: G Evaluation
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the date on which the report under subparagraph (F)(ii) is submitted, and every 4 years thereafter, the Secretary shall conduct, and make available to the public and the relevant committees of Congress, an independent review of the progress of the grants awarded under subparagraph (D) in meeting the recommendations and targets included in the report.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: l Cost sharing
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out the programs established under this section in accordance with .section 16352 of this title

children.heading: Merit review of proposals
children.label: m Merit review of proposals
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out the programs established under subsections (i), (j), and (k) in accordance with .section 16353 of this title

children.heading: Coordination and nonduplication
children.label: n Coordination and nonduplication
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall coordinate activities under this section with other programs and laboratories of the Department and other Federal research programs.

children.heading: Review by National Academy of Sciences
children.label: o Review by National Academy of Sciences
children.text: On the business day that is 5 years after , the Secretary shall offer to enter into an arrangement with the National Academy of Sciences to assess the performance of the Department in carrying out this section.December 19, 2007
the basic research program under subsection (f) $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2018;
the applied research program under subsection (g) $80,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2018; and;2
the energy storage research center program under subsection (h) $100,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2018;
the energy storage systems demonstration program under subsection (i) $30,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2018;
the vehicle energy storage demonstration program under subsection (j) $30,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2018; and
the electric drive vehicle battery recycling and second-life applications program under subsection (k) $200,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.heading: Critical material recycling and reuse research, development, and demonstration program
children.label: q Critical material recycling and reuse research, development, and demonstration program

children.heading: Critical material
children.label: A Critical material
children.text: The term “critical material” has the meaning given the term in 1606 of title 30.

children.heading: Critical material recycling
children.label: B Critical material recycling
children.text: The term “critical material recycling” means the separation and recovery of critical materials embedded within an energy storage system through physical or chemical means for the purpose of reuse of those critical materials in other technologies.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 2 Establishment
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish a research, development, and demonstration program for critical material recycling and reuse of energy storage systems containing critical materials.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: technologies, process improvements, and design optimizations that facilitate and promote critical material recycling of energy storage systems, including separation and sorting of component materials of such systems, and extraction, recovery, and reuse of critical materials from such systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: technologies and methods that mitigate emissions and environmental impacts that arise from critical material recycling, including disposal of toxic reagents and byproducts related to critical material recycling processes;

children.label: iii
children.text: technologies to enable extraction, recovery, and reuse of energy storage systems from electric vehicles and critical material recycling from such vehicles; and

children.label: iv
children.text: technologies and methods to enable the safe transport, storage, and disposal of energy storage systems containing critical materials, including waste materials and components recovered during the critical material recycling process; and

children.label: B
children.text: research on nontechnical barriers to improve the collection and critical material recycling of energy storage systems, including strategies to improve consumer education of, acceptance of, and participation in, the critical material recycling of energy storage systems.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 4 Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , and every 3 years thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate a report summarizing the activities, findings, and progress of the program.December 27, 2020
